name: PLAYBOOK OF GOVERNMENT SCHEMES AND INITIATIVES FOR STARTUPS
description: A consolidated reference guide to facilitate awareness and access to Central Government schemes, programmes, initiatives, and support mechanisms for startups across sectors
eligibility: Startups across sectors and stages of growth
benefits: Access to government schemes, programmes, initiatives, and support mechanisms

name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade
description: Provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, and product testing
eligibility: Startups recognized by DPIIT, less than 2 years old, and with a viable business idea
benefits: Up to Rs. 50 lakhs for validation of idea, and up to Rs. 2 crores for scale-up

name: NIDHI-EIR
description: Grants to start Prototype and beyond: Ready to build
eligibility: Student or early-stage researcher / aspiring entrepreneur (not yet a registered startup)

name: 2 "What Do You Need?" Index
description: A table to find schemes based on the type of support needed

name: NM-ICPS
description: Scheme, BHARATI

name: iDEX SPARK
description: IN-SPACe Seed Fund
eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startups

name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
description: Seed fund disbursed through approved incubators for PoC, prototype, market entry and commercialisation
eligibility: DPIIT-recognised early-stage startups with technology-enabled business ideas
benefits: Up to ₹20 lakh (grant); up to ₹50 lakh (convertible debt)

name: Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS)
description: Govt-backed fund-of-funds committing capital to SEBI-registered AIFs which then invest in startups
eligibility: SEBI-registered AIFs
benefits: Indirect investment in growth-stage startups through supported venture capital funds

name: Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0
description: Follow-on fund-of-funds to deepen domestic venture capital for deep tech, manufacturing and growth-stage startups
eligibility: Startups indirectly through SEBI-registered AIFs

name: Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS)
description: Credit Support Guarantee cover to lenders for collateral-free loans extended to eligible DPIIT-recognised startups
eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startups borrowing from eligible banks, NBFCs, AIFs
benefits: Credit guarantee cover up to ₹20 crore per eligible borrower, reduces lender risk
funding: Up to ₹20 crore per eligible borrower

name: Startups Intellectual Property Protection (SIPP) Scheme
description: Facilitated IPR filing scheme where government pays the facilitator’s professional fee on behalf of startups
eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startups filing patents, trademarks or designs in India or abroad
benefits: End-to-end IP facilitation at no charge to startup

name: GeM Startup Runway
description: Dedicated marketplace window on GeM enabling startups to sell innovative products/services directly to government buyers
eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startups. Also open to all startups for listing
benefits: Exemption from prior experience, turnover and EMD requirements. Platform for showcasing innovations for public procurement

name: NIDHI – Seed Support Program (SSP)
description: Seed support deployed through DST-supported TBIs to help incubated startups bridge prototype to market
eligibility: Technology startups incubated in eligible NIDHI/DST-supported TBIs
benefits: Seed funding (debt / equity / convertible instruments as per incubator)
funding: Up to ₹1 crore per startup, subject to TBI guidelines

name: NIDHI – PRAYAS 2.0
description: Prototype grant programme helping innovators convert technology ideas into working prototypes through PRAYAS Centres.
eligibility: Individual innovators and early-stage startups with technology-based ideas and prototype plans.
funding: Up to ₹40 lakh through PRAYAS 2.0 calls

name: Research, Development & Innovation (RDI) Scheme
description: Deep-Tech Scaling Large RDI financing framework providing long-term, low-cost capital for private-sector research and innovation
eligibility: Startups, companies and innovation-led enterprises in strategic/deep-tech sectors
benefits: Long-term R&D finance, Long-term financing / loans / equity-like support through designated channels
funding: Scheme corpus ₹1 lakh crore; project-level support varies

name: National Quantum Mission (NQM)
description: National mission offering rolling startup grants through 4 Thematic Hubs
eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startups in quantum computing, communication, sensing/metrology and materials/devices
benefits: R&D grant + incubation, testbed access and ecosystem mentoring via T-Hubs
funding: Up to ₹25 crore per startup; mission outlay ₹6,003.65 crore (FY2024–FY2031)

name: PRISM
description: Supporting individual innovators, startups and MSMEs to develop prototypes and commercialise innovations
eligibility: Individual innovators, startups and MSMEs with technology/product innovation ideas
funding: Up to ₹50 lakh depending on PRISM phase/category

name: Biotechnology Ignition Grant Scheme (BIG)
description: Translating innovative biotech ideas into proof-of-concept
eligibility: Individual innovators and early biotech startups with commercially promising ideas
funding: Up to ₹50 lakh for up to 18 months

name: Biotechnology Innovation Fund – Accelerating Entrepreneurs (AcE)
description: BIRAC equity fund-of-funds to accelerate biotech enterprises through SEBI-registered daughter funds
eligibility: High-potential biotech startups and SMEs seeking institutional venture capital
benefits: Equity investment via daughter funds (indirect funding)
funding: Startup-level investment varies; fund operates through selected AIFs

name: SEED Fund (SEED)
description: Seed Equity/equity-linked seed support deployed through BioNEST incubators for promising biotech startups
eligibility: BIRAC/BioNEST-incubated biotech startups needing early commercialisation capital
benefits: Up to ₹30 lakh per startup; incubator corpus typically ~₹2 crore

name: LEAP Fund
description: Equity/equity-linked fund for biotech startups ready to pilot or commercialise products
eligibility: Biotech startups incubated at selected LEAP/BioNEST fund partners

name: SPARSH
description: Supporting affordable and socially relevant healthcare/biotech product innovations
eligibility: Startups and innovators developing affordable products for priority public-health and social needs
funding: Up to ₹50 lakh, subject to call guidelines

name: AgriSURE
description: catalysing equity/debt capital for agri-tech, rural and allied-sector ventures
eligibility: Agri-tech startups, rural enterprises, FPO-linked innovations and value-chain ventures in agriculture
benefits: Equity up to ₹25 crore per startup

name: Innovation & Agri-Entrepreneurship Program (RKVY)
description: PM-RKVY agri-startup incubation programme supporting pre-seed and seed-stage agripreneurs through R-ABIs
eligibility: Agri and allied-sector startups/agripreneurs
funding: Up to ₹5 lakh (pre-seed); up to ₹25 lakh (seed stage)

name: SAMRIDH
description: Enabling accelerators to support promising software/product startups with services and matching funding
eligibility: Existing IT/software product startups
funding: Up to ₹40 lakh per startup via selected accelerators

name: GENESIS
description: Discovering, supporting and scaling technology startups in Tier-II/III cities via implementing agencies
eligibility: Technology startups and entrepreneurs in Tier-II/III locations, including deep-tech ventures
benefits: EiR, pilot, investment and deep-tech funding support through implementing agencies

name: STPI – Next Generation Incubation Scheme (NGIS)
ministry: Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY) / STPI
description: Supporting software product startups in selected Tier-II/III locations through incubation and seed support
eligibility: Tech/software product startups in identified NGIS cities

name: Chips to Startup (C2S)
description: R&D to Prototype programme for chip design, VLSI training and development of working silicon prototypes
eligibility: Academic institutions, startups/MSMEs and R&D organisations in semiconductor design
benefits: Design tools, mentoring, prototyping and project support
funding: Support varies under approved C2S project

name: Innovations for Defence Excellence (iDEX)
ministry: Ministry of Defence / Defence Innovation Organisation (DIO)
description: Challenge-led defence innovation platform connecting Startups, MSMEs and individual innovators solving defence problem statements
eligibility: Defence & aerospace startups/MSMEs with armed forces and defence PSUs
benefits: Prototype grant + user mentoring + procurement pathway
funding: Up to ₹10 crore (SPARK grants up to ₹1.5 crore)

name: ADITI
ministry: Ministry of Defence / Defence Innovation Organisation (DIO)
description: Acing Development of Innovative Technologies with iDEX sub-scheme for critical and strategic defence technologies requiring larger R&D investments
eligibility: Startups, MSMEs and innovators developing advanced deep-tech defence technologies
benefits: Up to ₹25 crore grant-in-aid per project, Large R&D grant + user engagement under iDEX framework
funding: Up to ₹25 crore grant-in-aid per project

name: Technology Development Fund (TDF)
description: Supporting indigenous defence technology development by industry, especially MSMEs/startups
eligibility: Indian industries, MSMEs and startups developing technologies for DRDO/Services needs
benefits: Grant-in-aid / project funding with DRDO mentoring
funding: Up to ₹10 crore per project, generally up to 90% of project cost

name: IN-SPACe Seed Fund Scheme
description: Encouraging startups and NGEs to develop space technologies and applications
eligibility: Indian space-tech startups and non-government entities developing space-sector products/applications
funding: Up to ₹1 crore per startup / NGE

name: Digital Communication Innovation Square (DCIS) Prototyping to Pilot Grant
description: Supporting indigenous telecom and ICT product innovation and commercialisation
eligibility: Startups, MSMEs, academia and innovators developing telecom/digital communication solutions
funding: Up to ₹50 lakh per selected proposal

name: GREAT – Grant for Research & Entrepreneurship across Aspiring Innovators in Technical Textiles
ministry: Ministry of Textiles (National Technical Textiles Mission)
description: Startup grant under National Technical Textiles Mission to convert prototypes into marketable technologies/products
eligibility: Individual innovators and startups in technical textiles, associated with approved incubators
benefits: Up to ₹50 lakh for up to 18 months, incubator support
keywords: Technical textiles, Grant, Startup, Innovators, Incubators

name: BHARATI
description: APEDA export-readiness accelerator for agri-food and agri-tech startups targeting global markets
eligibility: Agri-food and agri-tech startups, high-value producers and tech solution providers for exports
benefits: Export acceleration, mentoring, compliance and market-access support

name: S&T-PRISM
description: Supporting startups and MSMEs developing innovations in mining, mineral processing, metallurgy and recycling
eligibility: Early-stage startups in mining and metals industry
funding: Up to ₹2 crore per approved project

name: NIDHI – Entrepreneur in Residence (EIR) Ideation Fellowship
description: Fellowship letting aspiring entrepreneurs work full-time on technology business ideas before incorporation
eligibility: Graduate students and individual innovators with technology-based entrepreneurial ideas
funding: ₹10,000–₹30,000 per month for up to 12 months

name: NM-ICPS – Technology Innovation Hubs
description: Creating 25 Technology Innovation Hubs for R&D, translational research and startup incubation in CPS technologies
eligibility: Deep-tech startups, researchers and innovators in AI, IoT, robotics, cyber security and autonomous systems
benefits: Incubation, grants/seed support, testbeds, mentoring and market connects via TIHs

name: NIDHI-TBI
description: Support for establishing and strengthening technology business incubators for tech startups
eligibility: Host institutions/incubators supporting knowledge-based and technology-driven startups
benefits: Incubator-level support, grant to incubator + incubation services for startups

name: NIDHI-i-TBI
description: Creating inclusive technology incubators in underserved institutions/regions
eligibility: Underserved regions
benefits: Grant to incubator + maker-space/mentoring for startups

name: NIDHI – Accelerator
description: DST-supported accelerator mechanism to help incubated startups rapidly validate markets and scale
eligibility: Technology startups selected through supported incubators/accelerators
benefits: Structured acceleration, mentoring, investor connects and demo days
funding: Up to ₹40 lakh (₹30 lakh to accelerator; ₹10 lakh to startups)

name: NIDHI – Centres of Excellence Scaling (Incubation excellence)
description: Support for high-performing incubators to become Centres of Excellence and scale deep startup support
eligibility: Mature incubators with strong track record
benefits: High-impact incubator support, Large incubator grant, infrastructure, seed/scale support and networks
funding: Up to ₹50 crore over 5 years to incubator

name: BioAngels
description: Biotech angel investment and investor-connect platform curated for BIRAC-supported life-science startups
eligibility: Biotech, medtech, healthtech and life-sciences startups seeking angel capital
benefits: Investor connects / angel equity (private investment facilitation)
funding: Varies by angel round / investor decision

name: BioNEST Programme
description: Creating bio-incubators with wet labs, equipment, mentoring and commercialisation support
eligibility: Incubators, academic institutions and research parks supporting biotech startups

name: BIPP – Biotechnology Industry Partnership Programme
description: BIRAC partnership programme for high-risk, high-impact industry-led biotech innovation
eligibility: Biotech companies/startups and academia-industry consortia with transformational R&D proposals
benefits: Grant / soft loan / cost-sharing support for high-risk biotech R&D
funding: Project-based; varies by approved cost and scheme terms

name: E-YUVA
description: Empowering Youth for Value Added Innovative Translational Research Ideation
eligibility: Undergraduate/postgraduate students, fellows and young innovators

name: SITARE
description: Student innovation programme encouraging translational biotech ideas and early validation
eligibility: Students and young innovators with biotech/life-science innovations

name: PACE
description: Helping academic innovations move toward validation and commercial pathways
eligibility: Academic researchers, institutions and industry partners
benefits: Grant/Partnership support for validation and transnational research

name: SBIRI - Small Business Innovation Research Initiative
description: Public-private partnership scheme supporting biotech product development
eligibility: Biotech startups, SMEs and industry-led consortia undertaking innovative R&D
benefits: Grant up to ₹50 lakh; larger support as per call

name: TIDE 2.0
description: Incubator-led programme supporting ICT startups using emerging technologies for societal challenges
eligibility: ICT startups and entrepreneurs working in AI, IoT, blockchain, robotics and emerging technologies
benefits: PoC grants up to ₹7 lakh; larger support varies by centre/component

name: Design Linked Incentive (DLI) Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY) / C-DAC
description: Incentive and infrastructure support scheme for domestic companies, startups and MSMEs engaged in product design-linked incentive
eligibility: Semiconductor design companies, startups and MSMEs
benefits: Deployment incentive of 4-6% of net sales (capped at ₹30 crore)

name: Antariksh Venture Capital Fund (Space VCF)
description: Dedicated venture capital fund for Indian space-tech enterprises across the private space value chain
eligibility: Indian space-tech companies/startups

name: Technology Adoption Fund (TAF)
description: Fund moving space technologies from lab validation toward commercialisation and adoption
eligibility: Space startups, industry and NGEs advancing space technologies/applications
benefits: Grant / funding support for technology development and commercialisation

name: Credit Guarantee Trust Fund for Micro & Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises
description: Credit Support Guarantee scheme enabling collateral-free loans to micro and small enterprises through member lending institutions
eligibility: MSEs, including startup MSMEs, borrowing from eligible banks/NBFCs/FIs
benefits: Collateral-free MSME loans, Credit guarantee cover for lender, easier access to debt without collateral
funding: Guarantee coverage ceiling up to ₹10 crore

name: Self-Reliant India Fund (SRI Fund)
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises
description: Scaling Fund-of-funds for equity/quasi-equity infusion into growth-oriented MSMEs through daughter funds
eligibility: Viable MSMEs and startup MSMEs with growth potential

name: MSME Champions Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises
description: Umbrella MSME scheme including ZED, Lean and Innovative components to improve competitiveness
eligibility: MSMEs including startup MSMEs seeking quality, design, IP, lean or digital support
benefits: Subsidy / reimbursement / incubation and advisory support
funding: Varies under ZED, Lean and Innovative guidelines

name: International Cooperation Scheme Market Access MSME scheme
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises
description: Supporting participation in international exhibitions, trade fairs and buyer-seller meets
eligibility: MSMEs, industry associations and startup MSMEs seeking export/market linkages
benefits: Reimbursement/subsidy for travel, stall, registration and event costs
funding: Assistance varies by event category and guidelines

name: National SC-ST Hub Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises
description: Supporting SC/ST entrepreneurs through capacity building, procurement linkages and mentoring
eligibility: SC/ST-owned MSMEs and entrepreneurs, including startup MSMEs

name: Atal Tinkering Labs (ATL)
description: School innovation labs building creativity, design thinking and problem-solving skills among students
eligibility: Schools and students
funding: Up to ₹20 lakh per selected school over multiple years

name: Atal Incubation Centres (AICs)
description: Supporting world-class incubators across universities, institutions and private hosts
eligibility: Host institutions/incubators and startups
benefits: Incubation services, grant-in-aid to incubator, mentoring/network for startups

name: Atal Community Innovation Centres (ACICs)
description: Setting up community innovation centres in underserved regions
eligibility: Institutions/organisations supporting community innovators and early-stage startups
benefits: Grant support to host centres + incubation ecosystem
funding: Up to ₹2.5 crore over prescribed period

name: Established Incubation Centres (EICs)
description: Incubation support for established incubators to scale startup support capabilities
eligibility: Existing incubators and institutions supporting startups
benefits: Grant / capacity support to incubator; indirect startup support
funding: Up to ₹10 crore grant; matching private contribution required

name: Agriculture Infrastructure Financing Facility (AIF)
description: Scaling post-harvest and community farming assets
eligibility: Farmers
FPOs
Agri-entrepreneurs
Startups
Cooperatives

name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
description: Credit Support Collateral-free micro-credit programme under Shishu, Kishor and Tarun categories
eligibility: Non-corporate, non-farm micro/small enterprises including early-stage businesses and startups
benefits: Loans up to ₹10 lakh; Tarun Plus up to ₹20 lakh for eligible repeat borrowers
funding: Term loan / working capital through banks, MFIs and NBFCs

name: Stand Up India
description: Bank loan scheme for greenfield enterprises promoted by SC/ST and/or women entrepreneurs
eligibility: At least one SC/ST borrower or one woman borrower per bank branch for greenfield non-farm enterprise

name: PMFME
description: Formalising and upgrading micro food-processing enterprises under One District One Product
eligibility: Individual micro food-processing units, SHGs, FPOs, cooperatives and food startups/micro-enterprises
benefits: Credit-linked subsidy, seed capital for SHGs, branding/marketing and capacity support
funding: 35% credit-linked subsidy up to ₹10 lakh per unit; SHG seed capital up to ₹40,000/member

name: IFSCA Fintech Incentive Scheme
description: Incentive scheme to attract and support fintechs engaging with the IFSC ecosystem at GIFT City
eligibility: Indian and foreign fintech entities, startups and accelerators operating/engaging with GIFT IFSC
benefits: Grant / incentive support (Sandbox, PoC, listing, accelerator and special grants)
funding: Up to ₹75 lakh in many grant categories; varies by incentive type

name: Technology Development-cum-Incubation Centres
description: Translating DAE spin-off technologies into market-ready products
eligibility: Startups, MSMEs and entrepreneurs licensing/incubating DAE technologies
benefits: Technology transfer, lab access, scientist mentoring and incubation
funding: No standard grant; support is incubation/technology access based

name: Start-up Village Entrepreneurship Programme (SVEP)
description: Helping rural poor set up local enterprises through community enterprise support
eligibility: Rural entrepreneurs from SHG/NRLM ecosystem; relevant for rural startups/micro-enterprises
funding: Up to ₹6.5 crore per block overall

name: Venture Capital Fund for Scheduled Castes
description: Social sector venture fund providing concessionary finance to SC entrepreneurs and companies
eligibility: Companies promoted by Scheduled Caste entrepreneurs, including startups/MSMEs
funding: ₹10 lakh to ₹15 crore, subject to fund guidelines

name: STI Hubs for SC/ST Communities
description: Establishing STI hubs for technology-led livelihood and enterprise development in SC/ST communities
eligibility: Institutions/NGOs and community enterprises serving SC/ST communities
benefits: Inclusive technology deployment, project grants, technology deployment, training and enterprise support
funding: Project-based; amount varies by sanctioned STI hub
keywords: STI Hubs, SC/ST Communities, Livelihood Innovation, Technology Deployment, Enterprise Development

name: National Livestock Mission (NLM)
ministry: Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying
description: Promoting entrepreneurship in livestock sectors such as poultry, sheep, goat, piggery and fodder.
eligibility: Individuals, FPOs, SHGs, JLGs, Section 8 companies
benefits: Capital subsidy routed with loan/project finance depending on project category

name: Mission on Advanced & High Impact Research (MAHIR)
ministry: Ministry of Power & Ministry of New & Renewable Energy (MNRE)
description: Joint mission to identify, develop and demonstrate emerging power-sector technologies indigenously
eligibility: Startups, industry, academia and research institutions working on power-sector technologies
benefits: Power sector deep-tech R&D project funding, pilot support and commercialisation facilitation
funding: Project-based; mission funded through MoP/MNRE/CPSE pooled resources
keywords: GRANT, R&D, Power Sector, Deep-tech, Commercialisation

name: Yuva Sahakar – Cooperative Enterprise Support & Innovation Scheme
description: Supporting newly formed innovative cooperative societies and young cooperative entrepreneurs
eligibility: Cooperative societies in operation for minimum period as per scheme; youth-led/new cooperatives
funding: Loan up to project norms; often up to ₹3 crore project cost reference

name: Entrepreneurship & Skill Development Programme (ESDP)
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises
description: Capacity Building MSME training programme building entrepreneurial and managerial skills for prospective entrepreneurs.
eligibility: Prospective and existing entrepreneurs, MSMEs, youth and startups needing business skills.
benefits: Training cost supported per programme norms

name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
description: Provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry and commercialisation.
eligibility: DPIIT recognised startups, Incorporated not more than 2 years ago, Startup must have a business idea to develop a product or a service with market fit, viable commercialisation, and scope of scaling.
benefits: Up to ₹20 lakh as grant for validation of Proof of Concept, or prototype development, or product trials, Up to ₹50 lakh of investment for market entry, commercialisation, or scaling up through convertible debentures or debt or debt-linked instruments

name: Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS)
ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
description: Provides capital to venture funds to invest in startups, helping increase the availability of funding and support the growth of India’s startup ecosystem.
eligibility: SEBI-registered AIFs, DPIIT recognised startups
benefits: The Scheme has a corpus of ₹10,000 crore, which is aimed to be provided to startup through AIFs.
keywords: Fund of Funds, Startups, Venture Capital

name: Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0
ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
description: Supports startups by channeling government capital into venture funds to boost investment in emerging businesses, aiming to fuel innovation in priority sectors.
eligibility: SEBI-registered AIFs, DPIIT recognised startups
benefits: The Scheme has a corpus of ₹10,000 crore, which is aimed to be provided to startup through AIFs.
keywords: Startup India, Fund of Funds, Scaling

name: Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS)
ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
description: Helps startups access loans by assuring lenders that the government will cover part of the risk, making it easier for young businesses to get funding without collateral.
eligibility: DPIIT recognised startups, Eligible (i) Scheduled Commercial Banks and Financial Institutions; (ii) RBI registered Non-Banking Finance Companies (NBFCs) and (iii) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs)
benefits: Maximum guarantee of ₹20 crore or actual outstanding credit amount, whichever is less.

name: Scheme for Facilitating Startups Intellectual Property Protection (SIPP)
ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
description: Helps startups protect their ideas by providing support and cost relief for filing patents, trademarks, and designs, encouraging innovation and safeguarding ownership.
eligibility: DPIIT recognised startups, Applicants must self-declare that they have not availed funds under any other government scheme for paying facilitator/patent agent/trademark agent fees for the same IP application.
benefits: Support for filing and processing patent, trademark and design applications, Professional fees of facilitators are paid/reimbursed by the office of CGPDTM; startups generally bear only statutory government fees.

name: GeM Startup Runway
description: Enables startups to showcase and sell their innovative products directly to government buyers, helping them access public procurement opportunities and grow their business.
eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startups registered as sellers/service providers on GeM, Startups with products/services in existing GeM categories or in dedicated startup sectors
benefits: Access to a large base of government buyers across ministries, departments, PSUs and other public entities, Exemption/relaxation from requirements such as prior turnover, prior experience, and Earnest Money Deposit (EMD).

name: NIDHI – Seed Support Program (SSP)
description: Provides early-stage funding and support to innovators to help them develop and validate their ideas, encouraging the creation of viable startups.
eligibility: Incubatee should be a DPIIT recognised startup with minimum of three months of residency at the STEPs/TBIs.
benefits: Under the program, the startups are supported primarily for product development, testing and trials, test marketing, mentoring, professional consultancy.

name: NIDHI – Promotion and Acceleration of Young and Aspiring Technology Entrepreneurs (PRAYAS) 2.0
description: Provides early-stage funding and prototyping support to young innovators, helping them turn their technology ideas into working products and viable startups.
eligibility: A DPIIT recognised startup or an individual innovator are eligible to apply under the program.
benefits: Level 1 – PCs: Each innovator/startup supported by a Level 1 Centre PC may receive up to ₹20 Lakhs grant for prototyping, Level 2 – APCs: Provides prototyping grants of up to ₹40 lakh per innovator/ startup.

name: Research, Development & Innovation (RDI) Scheme
description: Provides funding and support to help organizations create and improve new technologies or ideas so they can boost innovation, economic growth, and global competitiveness.
eligibility: The RDI Fund is provided to Second Level Fund Managers (SLFM) which include Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs), Development Finance Institutions (DFIs), Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs), and Focused Research Organisations (FROs).
benefits: Loan – Debt funding (via loan or optionally convertible debt) from the SLFM, calculated as 50% of the project cost, Equity – Funding via equity or equity linked instruments from the SLFM, calculated as 50% of the value of each funding round.

name: National Quantum Mission (NQM)
description: Supports research and development of advanced quantum technologies (like quantum computing and secure communication) so India can build cutting-edge capabilities and stay competitive in future tech.
eligibility: Academic institutions, R&D labs, and consortia working in quantum computing, communication, sensing/metrology, and materials/devices, Startups working in quantum technology, preferably DPIIT-registered and Indian-owned/controlled as per startup support guidelines.
benefits: Funding and institutional support for quantum R&D, prototypes, product development, and commercialisation through NQM T-Hubs.

name: Promoting Innovations in Individuals, Start-ups and MSMEs (PRISM)
description: Provides funding and support to individual innovators, startups, and small businesses so they can turn new ideas into useful products and grow their businesses.
eligibility: Public funded institutions or organizations viz. Autonomous Organizations or Society registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860 or Indian Trusts Act, 1882 engaged in promotion of innovation, Any Indian citizen having an innovative idea and wish to translate their idea into working prototypes/models/processes.
benefits: Support of ₹12 lakh per annum would be given to TOCICs to cover expenses, For innovators: Phase I: Up to ₹2 lakh or 90% of the approved project cost, Phase II: Up to ₹50 lakh or 50% of the approved project cost

name: Biotechnology Ignition Grant Scheme (BIG)
ministry: Department of Biotechnology / Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC)
description: Provides early-stage funding and support to biotech innovators and startups so they can turn promising scientific ideas into viable products and businesses.
eligibility: Individual Entrepreneurs and startups, The applicant must be incubated in an incubator (may/may not be supported by BIRAC).
benefits: Grant-in-aid of up to ₹50 lakh provided to startup for a period of up to 18 months, During the BIG tenure, BIRAC extends several networking opportunities to connect with experts, mentors, industry, investors, peer to peer learning, specialized trainings & workshops, exposure to various platforms on merit basis

name: Biotechnology Innovation Fund – Accelerating Entrepreneurs (AcE)
ministry: Department of Biotechnology / Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC)
description: Provides financial support to biotech startups to develop and scale innovative solutions
eligibility: SEBI-registered AIFs, startups and SMEs in biotech sector
benefits: Up to ₹30 crore funding, ticket size up to ₹7 crore

name: BIRAC Incubator SEED Fund (SEED)
ministry: Department of Biotechnology / Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC)
description: Provides early-stage funding to biotech startups through incubators
eligibility: Biotech/Med-Tech/Life Sciences startups

name: LEAP Fund – Launching Entrepreneurial Driven Affordable Products
description: Supports entrepreneurs in creating affordable, innovative products
eligibility: Startups in Biotech/med-tech/life science sectors

name: SPARSH – Social Innovation Programme for Products Affordable & Relevant to Social Health
description: Supports innovators in developing affordable health-related solutions
eligibility: Biotechnology Indian startups, LLPs, Indian academic scientists

name: AgriSURE – Agri Fund for Startups & Rural Enterprises
description: Supports agri-startups and rural enterprises with funding and resources
eligibility: Startups working in agriculture and rural development sectors

name: Innovation & Agri-Entrepreneurship Program (RKVY)
description: Supports agri-startups with funding, mentorship, and incubation
eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startups

name: SAMRIDH – Startup Accelerators of MeitY for Product Innovation, Development & Growth
description: Supports startups by funding and accelerating innovative tech products
eligibility: Accelerators, IT-based startups

name: GENESIS – Gen-Next Support for Innovative Startups
description: Supports early-stage startups with funding, support, and guidance
eligibility: Entities registered under Section 8, Incubators, Accelerators

name: STPI – Next Generation Incubation Scheme (NGIS)
ministry: Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY) / STPI
description: Helps nurture early-stage tech startups with incubation support and funding
eligibility: Indian startups working on software product development

name: Chips to Startup (C2S) Programme
ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
description: Supports development of semiconductor/chip-design ecosystem
eligibility: Academic institutions, R&D organisations, startups, MSMEs

name: Innovations for Defence Excellence (iDEX)
ministry: Ministry of Defence / Defence Innovation Organisation (DIO)
description: Supports startups and innovators in building defence solutions
eligibility: DPIIT recognised startups, MSMEs, individual innovators

name: Acing Development of Innovative Technologies with iDEX (ADITI)
ministry: Ministry of Defence / Defence Innovation Organisation (DIO)
description: Supports startups and innovators in building new defence and aerospace technologies
eligibility: DPIIT recognised startups, Indian companies, individual innovators

name: Technology Development Fund (TDF)
description: Supports Indian companies and startups in developing defence technologies
eligibility: Public limited companies, private limited companies, partnership firms, LLPs

name: IN-SPACe Seed Fund Scheme
description: Supports early-stage space startups with grant funding and mentorship
eligibility: DPIIT recognised early-stage startups

name: Digital Communication Innovation Square (DCIS)
description: Supports startups and innovators in developing new digital communication technologies
eligibility: DPIIT recognised startups, Indian companies, individual innovators

name: GREAT – Grant for Research & Entrepreneurship across Aspiring Innovators in Technical Textiles
ministry: Ministry of Textiles (National Technical Textiles Mission)
description: Supports innovators and startups in technical textiles with funding and incubator support
eligibility: Individual innovators, startup companies, projects related to technical-textile areas

name: BHARATI – Bharat’s Hub for Agritech, Resilience, Advancement & Incubation for Export Innovation
description: Supports agri-food and agritech startups with acceleration support and market access
eligibility: Agri-food startups, technology and service startups, solution providers for SPS challenges
benefits: Acceleration support, access to APEDA’s ecosystem

name: S&T-PRISM – Promotion of Research & Innovation in Mining, Minerals & Recycling Sector
description: Supports early-stage startups in mining, minerals, metals, and recycling with funding and incubation support
eligibility: Startups operating at the early stages in the mining and metal industry
